# WebWaka OS — Verticals Execution Prompts: Commerce P3 Tail

**Document type:** Agent execution prompt set
**Scope:** Commerce P3 verticals — 15 verticals covered
**Repo:** https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os
**Branch base:** `main`
**Milestone targets:** M10–M12
**Status:** Ready for agent execution — all P1 verticals complete (M8b–M8e)
**Generated by:** MASTER_CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md — Set C

---

> **3-in-1 Platform Note:**
> P3 verticals primarily serve **Pillar 1 (Ops)** and **Pillar 3 (Marketplace/Discovery)**.
> Most P3 informal-economy verticals use a simplified 3-state FSM (`seeded` → `claimed` → `active`) and require only Tier 1 KYC — serving the broadest possible informal sector base.
> **SuperAgent AI is cross-cutting — it is NOT a fourth pillar.** AI at L1 autonomy (informational only) for P3 informal verticals.
> See `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md` for the full pillar map.

### General rules for all agents using these prompts

- Never make assumptions about WebWaka's architecture, API contracts, or business logic. Always read the referenced documents and code first.
- Research deeply before executing. When encountering domain-specific patterns, do online research first and synthesise with Nigeria market context.
- P3 verticals must be fully functional at Tier 1 KYC — they serve the informal economy; do not over-engineer the regulatory gates.
- Thoroughness is far more important than speed.
- All work must be pushed to GitHub. No local partial work should remain outside the repo.
- SuperAgent is the AI layer — all AI features route through `packages/superagent`. Never call AI providers directly.
- Platform Invariants are non-negotiable. Read `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` in full before implementing any vertical.
- Every monetary value must be an integer in kobo (P9). No decimals, no floats.
- No cross-tenant data leakage (T3). Every D1 query scopes to `tenant_id`.

---

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C1: Artisanal Mining Operator Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `artisanal-mining`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigeria has significant artisanal and small-scale mining (ASM) in Zamfara, Plateau, Niger, Kaduna states; gold, tin, columbite, gemstones
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:**
  - Verticals master plan: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md
  - 3-in-1 platform architecture: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md
  - Verticals FSM engine: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/packages/verticals/src/
  - Platform invariants: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/platform-invariants.md
  - Entitlement model: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/entitlement-model.md
  - AI integration framework: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md
  - SuperAgent product spec: https://github.com/WebWakaDOS/webwaka-os/blob/main/docs/governance/superagent/02-product-spec.md

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in extractive industry operations management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:**
- Nigerian artisanal and small-scale mining (ASM) — MMSD (Mining Cadastre Office), Zamfara gold rush, Jos Plateau tin/columbite, Minas Gerais-style cooperative mining
- Mining permit registration, production volume tracking (kg/tonnes as integers), mineral buyer/offtaker management
- Cloudflare Workers + Hono + D1 TypeScript strict mode; WebWaka SuperAgent integration; Verticals FSM engine

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `artisanal-mining`
- `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`
- `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md` — P2, P9, P10, P12, P13, T3, T4
- `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv` — row for `artisanal-mining`
- `packages/verticals/src/` — FSM engine; `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`
- `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/` — next migration number

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian ASM sector — MMSD permit categories, Zamfara gold volume, Plateau tin producers, artisanal miner cooperative structures; competitors: none (no Nigerian ASM management SaaS); key features: mining site register, production log (mineral type, weight_grams as integer, quality_grade, sale_price_kobo), buyer/offtaker management, permit validity tracking, cooperative member roster.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian artisanal miners a digital production log, offtaker management, and permit compliance tracker
- **Schema:** `artisanal_mining_profiles` (company/cooperative name, MMSD_permit, mineral_type, state, LGA, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `mining_production_log` (site_id, mineral_type, weight_grams INTEGER, quality_grade, sale_price_kobo, offtaker_name, sale_date, tenant_id); `mining_permits` (permit_number, permit_type, valid_from, valid_until, state, tenant_id)
- **FSM states:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `mmsd_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_FORECAST` (mineral price trend advisory — L1 informational) — NDPR consent; USSD excluded (P12)
- **Regulatory hook:** MMSD permit number at `mmsd_verified`; CBN FX awareness for export proceeds
- **KYC:** Tier 2 minimum (high-value commodity transactions)

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-artisanal-mining` from `main`.

**3a. Schema:** `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_artisanal_mining.sql`. Weight in integer grams (no floats). All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3).

**3b. Package:** `packages/verticals-artisanal-mining/src/index.ts` — `registerArtisanalMiningVertical()`. `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate mineral type counts and revenue totals — no offtaker names (P13). L1 autonomy only.

**3c. Routes:** `apps/api/src/routes/verticals/artisanal-mining.ts`. Production log CRUD, permit management. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

**3d. T3:** All queries scoped to `tenant_id`.

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-artisanal-mining/src/artisanal-mining.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `mmsd_verified` with permit; production log with integer weight_grams and sale_price_kobo (P9); permit expiry alert; AI price advisory with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: float weight rejected; fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/artisanal-mining): Artisanal Mining vertical — production log, MMSD compliance, AI price advisory (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-artisanal-mining
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled `3in1:pillar-1/3`, guards, SuperAgent L1, T3, ≥15 tests, zero typecheck/lint errors.

---

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C2: Borehole Drilling Company Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `borehole-driller`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Critical infrastructure service in water-scarce Nigerian communities; thousands of drilling companies operating nationwide
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (same standard links as above)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in water infrastructure project management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian borehole drilling sector — state water board compliance, COREN engineering registration for drilling contractors, rural/urban borehole demand patterns; project-based billing in kobo, rig deployment scheduling.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

- `docs/governance/verticals-master-plan.md` — entry for `borehole-driller`; `docs/governance/3in1-platform-architecture.md`; `docs/governance/platform-invariants.md`; `infra/db/seeds/0004_verticals-master.csv`; `packages/verticals/src/`; `packages/auth/src/guards.ts`; `docs/governance/ai-integration-framework.md`; `infra/db/migrations/`

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian borehole drilling market — NGO-funded community boreholes vs private residential boreholes, average depth and cost in naira (× 100 for kobo), state water board approval requirements, COREN licensing for geotechnical contractors.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian borehole drilling companies a digital project register, rig deployment tracker, state water board compliance records, and client payment schedule
- **Schema:** `borehole_driller_profiles` (company_name, COREN_number, state_water_board_reg, CAC_RC, rig_count, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `borehole_projects` (client_phone, location_address, state, depth_metres INTEGER, casing_type, total_cost_kobo, deposit_kobo, balance_kobo, water_board_approval_number, status (survey/drilling/casing/pump_test/handover/completed), tenant_id); `borehole_rigs` (rig_name, rig_capacity_metres INTEGER, current_project_id, status (available/deployed/maintenance), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `coren_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (project pipeline revenue) — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for project billing above ₦500,000

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-borehole-driller` from `main`. Schema: `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_borehole_driller.sql` — depth and capacity as integers (no floats); monetary in kobo (P9); `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Package: `registerBoreholdeDrillerVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Routes: project CRUD, rig management. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-borehole-driller/src/borehole-driller.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `coren_verified`; project with integer depth_metres, total_cost_kobo, deposit_kobo (P9); rig status management; AI revenue forecast with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: float depth rejected; fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/borehole-driller): Borehole Driller vertical — projects, rig management, water board compliance (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-borehole-driller
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C3: Building Materials Supplier Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `building-materials`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Cement, iron rods, roofing sheets, tiles — major distribution sector across Onitsha, Apapa, Kano, Minna building material markets
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in building materials wholesale and retail management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian building materials sector — Onitsha Bridgehead cement market, Apapa iron rods, Kano tiles cluster, SON product standards for cement and steel; bulk invoice management, contractor credit accounts.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading (100% before execution):**

Same standard documents — `verticals-master-plan.md`, `3in1-platform-architecture.md`, `platform-invariants.md`, seed CSV, FSM engine, guards, AI framework, next migration number.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian building materials market — cement volumes (Dangote, BUA, Lafarge retail distribution), average contractor credit terms, Onitsha/Apapa market volume; competitors: no dedicated SaaS, primarily phone orders and manual invoicing; key features: product catalogue (cement bags, iron rods per tonne, roofing sheets per bundle), contractor credit account management, bulk invoice in kobo, delivery truck dispatch, SON product batch tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian building materials dealers a digital product catalogue, contractor credit account, bulk invoice management, and stock tracking — all in kobo
- **Schema:** `building_materials_profiles` (company_name, CAC_RC, SON_dealer_number, market_cluster, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `building_materials_catalogue` (product_name, category (cement/steel/roofing/tiles/glass/paint/sanitary), unit (bag/tonne/bundle/m2/litre), unit_price_kobo, quantity_in_stock, tenant_id); `building_materials_orders` (client_phone, client_name, order_items JSON, total_kobo, credit_account_id, delivery_address, status (placed/confirmed/dispatched/delivered/invoiced/settled), tenant_id); `contractor_credit_accounts` (contractor_phone, contractor_name, credit_limit_kobo, balance_owing_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (cement/steel demand by season), `SALES_FORECAST` — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for contractor credit accounts; Tier 3 for bulk procurement contracts above ₦5M

---

**3. Implementation workflow:**

Branch: `feat/vertical-building-materials`. Schema: `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_building_materials.sql`. All monetary in kobo (P9). `tenant_id NOT NULL` (T3). Package: `registerBuildingMaterialsVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate product sales by category — no contractor PII (P13). Routes: catalogue + orders + credit accounts CRUD. AI blocked on USSD (P12).

---

**4. QA and verification:**

**Test file:** `packages/verticals-building-materials/src/building-materials.test.ts`

Positive: register; FSM to `cac_verified`; catalogue item with integer unit_price_kobo; order with integer total_kobo; credit account with integer credit_limit_kobo; AI demand forecast with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo (P9); cross-tenant → 403 (T3); AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error (T4). Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403.

**Minimum test count: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/building-materials): Building Materials vertical — catalogue, contractor credit, AI demand (M12)"
git push origin feat/vertical-building-materials
```
PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent L1, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Ubiquitous in Nigerian urban areas; typically informal, local government permit only

- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in automotive services and informal sector digitisation, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian car wash sector — informal roadside operations, local government permit, daily cash flow management, loyalty card concepts; Tier 1 KYC focus; simplified 3-state FSM.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents as listed above.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: car wash market in Nigerian cities — numbers, average price (₦500–₦5,000 per wash), weekly revenue; competitors: none dedicated; WhatsApp-based; features: vehicle log (plate number, wash type, price_kobo), daily revenue tally, loyalty tracker (every 10th wash free), water supply tracking.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Digitise the simplest Nigerian car wash — vehicle log, daily revenue, loyalty programme
- **Schema:** `car_wash_profiles` (business_name, lg_permit_number, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `car_wash_visits` (vehicle_plate, wash_type (basic/full/detailing), price_kobo, visit_date, loyalty_count, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state informal)
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (daily revenue trend) — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 sufficient

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-car-wash`. Schema: `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_car_wash.sql`. All kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). Package: `registerCarWashVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. L1 AI cap; USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** `packages/verticals-car-wash/src/car-wash.test.ts`. Positive: register; FSM 3-state; visit with integer price_kobo (P9); loyalty count increment; AI trend with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/car-wash): Car Wash vertical — vehicle log, loyalty programme, AI revenue trend (M12)"`. PR checklist: FSM (3-state), migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, Tier 1 KYC, SuperAgent L1, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in corporate facilities management software, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian facilities management sector — post-construction cleaning, industrial cleaning, government contract procurement (BPP); FMENV compliance for chemical waste disposal; multi-site contract management in kobo.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian corporate cleaning/FM market — Lagos corporate buildings, government ministries as clients, average monthly FM contract value; competitors: Philips Consulting FM division, local FM companies (manual); features: multi-site client contracts (monthly_fee_kobo), staff roster per site, compliance inspection log, chemical/consumable stock, BPP registration for government contracts, FMENV waste disposal certificate.

Execution plan:
- **Objective:** Give Nigerian facility management companies digital multi-site contract management, staff deployment, consumable stock tracking, and FMENV compliance records
- **Schema:** `cleaning_company_profiles` (company_name, CAC_RC, BPP_registration, FMENV_cert, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `fm_contracts` (client_name, client_phone, sites_count, monthly_fee_kobo, contract_start, contract_end, status (active/paused/expired), tenant_id); `fm_staff_deployments` (contract_id, staff_name, site_name, shift_type, monthly_salary_kobo, tenant_id); `fm_supplies` (supply_name, quantity, unit_cost_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `cac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (contract renewal pipeline) — L2; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for monthly payroll ops; Tier 3 for government contracts above ₦5M

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-cleaning-company`. Schema: `[NEXT_NUMBER]_vertical_cleaning_company.sql`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). Package: `registerCleaningCompanyVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. AI: aggregate contract revenue — no staff PII (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Positive: register; FSM to `cac_verified`; contract with integer monthly_fee_kobo; deployment with integer salary_kobo; supply with integer unit_cost_kobo; AI pipeline with aiRights + NDPR. Negative: fractional kobo; cross-tenant → 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM → typed error. Security: unauth → 401; KYC → 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/cleaning-company): Cleaning Company FM vertical — contracts, staff deployment, FMENV compliance (M11)"`. PR checklist: FSM, migration, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`, labeled, guards, SuperAgent, T3, ≥15 tests, zero errors.

Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in informal beauty and grooming services digitisation, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian barbing/hair salon informal sector — walk-in queues, no appointments, daily cash management, local business permit; Tier 1 KYC; 3-state FSM for informal operators.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: service price list (haircut types, braiding styles, prices in kobo), daily revenue tally, walk-in customer count log, local permit tracking. Simpler than `beauty-salon` — no appointment booking system required.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `hair_salon_profiles` (salon_name, type (barbing/braiding/mixed), lg_permit_number, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `hair_salon_services` (service_name, price_kobo, tenant_id); `hair_salon_daily_log` (log_date, customers_served, revenue_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state informal)
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (daily revenue trend) — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 sufficient

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-hair-salon`. `registerHairSalonVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). L1 AI. USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM 3-state; service with integer price_kobo; daily log with integer revenue_kobo; AI trend with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/hair-salon): Hair Salon/Barbing vertical — price list, daily log, AI revenue trend (M10)"`. PR checklist: 3-state FSM, Tier 1 KYC, `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`, labeled, SuperAgent L1, T3, ≥15 tests.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C9: Petrol Station (DPR/NUPRC-licensed) Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `petrol-station`

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in petrol retail operations for smaller-scale stations, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian downstream petroleum — NUPRC retail licence for smaller outlets, SON pump calibration compliance, daily cash reconciliation for independent stations; lighter-weight feature set than the full `fuel-station` P2 vertical.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features (simplified vs P2 fuel-station): daily sales log (PMS/AGO litres × current price kobo), cash received vs expected reconciliation, NUPRC licence tracking, basic stock level alert.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `petrol_station_profiles` (station_name, NUPRC_licence, state, lga, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `petrol_daily_sales` (sale_date, product (PMS/AGO/DPK), litres_sold INTEGER, price_per_litre_kobo, total_kobo, cash_received_kobo, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nuprc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `SALES_FORECAST` (daily litre trend) — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 2 for cash reconciliation operations

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-petrol-station`. `registerPetrolStationVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Integer litres, integer price_per_litre_kobo, integer total_kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `nuprc_verified`; daily sale with integer values (P9); reconciliation check; AI trend with aiRights + NDPR; float litres rejected; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/petrol-station): Petrol Station P3 vertical — daily sales, NUPRC compliance, AI trend (M11)"`. PR checklist standard.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C10: Phone Repair & Accessories Shop Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `phone-repair-shop`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Alongside electronics-repair (Set A C7), this specifically covers the phone repair + accessories retail combination ubiquitous in Nigerian markets; NCC type-approval awareness
- **Milestone target:** M10
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in phone repair and mobile accessories retail management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian mobile phone repair sector — Computer Village Lagos, Slot (repair arm), local repair shops across all states; NCC type-approval awareness for accessories; accessories retail alongside repair services; IMEI tracking (stored securely, never passed to AI).

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Key features: repair job card (device model, IMEI stored securely, fault, labour_kobo, parts_kobo), accessories stock (screen protectors, cases, chargers by brand, unit_price_kobo), accessories retail POS (sale items, total_kobo), daily revenue split (repair vs accessories), WhatsApp-style customer notification reference.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `phone_repair_profiles` (shop_name, CAC_or_trade_number, location_cluster, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `phone_repair_jobs` (device_brand, device_model, imei TEXT, fault, labour_kobo, parts_kobo, total_kobo, status (intake/repairing/completed/collected), warranty_days, customer_phone, tenant_id); `phone_accessories_stock` (product_name, brand, category (case/screen_guard/charger/cable/earpiece/other), unit_price_kobo, quantity_in_stock, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `active` (3-state; or `cac_verified` if registered)
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (accessories by brand demand), `SALES_FORECAST` — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for repair invoicing; Tier 2 for accessories wholesale above ₦200,000/day
- **CRITICAL P13:** IMEI field must never be passed to AI layer — filter before any SuperAgent call

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-phone-repair-shop`. `registerPhoneRepairShopVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. Kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). IMEI strictly excluded from all AI calls (P13). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM; job with integer labour_kobo, parts_kobo (P9); accessories item with integer unit_price_kobo; AI demand with aiRights + NDPR; verify IMEI not passed to SuperAgent (P13); fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/phone-repair-shop): Phone Repair/Accessories vertical — job cards, accessories stock, AI demand (M10)"`. PR checklist: P13 IMEI exclusion explicitly verified.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C14: Used Car Dealer / Auto Trader Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `used-car-dealer`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigeria's used car market is massive; Berger, Cotonou cars (ECOWAS imports), Tokunbo market; FRSC VIN verification, state motor dealers association
- **Milestone target:** M12
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 2 (Branding) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","branding","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in used vehicle retail management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian used car market — Berger (Lagos), Trade Fair Complex, Cotonou-to-Nigeria import pipeline, FRSC VIN verification, customs duty and import permits; high-value transactions requiring Tier 3 KYC; Pillar 2 branded car listing site.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian used car market size — Autochek, Cars45, Cheki.com.ng vehicle listings; FRSC VIN verification process; average transaction value (₦1.5M–₦25M); key features: vehicle listing (make, model, year, mileage, VIN, asking_price_kobo, condition, photos reference), buyer enquiry pipeline, offer/negotiation tracking, FRSC VIN verification flag, customs duty reference for Cotonou imports.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `used_car_dealer_profiles` (dealership_name, CAC_RC, state_motor_dealers_assoc, FRSC_dealer_reg, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `used_car_listings` (make, model, year INTEGER, mileage_km INTEGER, vin TEXT, condition (excellent/good/fair), asking_price_kobo, customs_cleared BOOLEAN, status (available/reserved/sold), tenant_id); `used_car_enquiries` (listing_id, buyer_phone, offer_price_kobo, status (new/negotiating/agreed/completed/cancelled), tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `frsc_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `PROPERTY_VALUATION_ASSIST` (vehicle market price benchmarking) — L2; Growth plan; NDPR consent
- **KYC:** Tier 3 mandatory — vehicle transactions are high-value; CBN cash-above-threshold rules apply
- **CRITICAL:** VIN must never be passed to AI layer (P13)

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-used-car-dealer`. `registerUsedCarDealerVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`. All prices in kobo (P9). Mileage and year as integers. VIN excluded from AI (P13). Tenant scoped (T3). USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `frsc_verified`; listing with integer asking_price_kobo, mileage_km, year (P9); enquiry with offer_price_kobo; verify VIN not passed to SuperAgent (P13); AI valuation with aiRights + NDPR; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; Tier 3 KYC enforced → 403 for lower tiers. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Push:** `git commit -m "feat(vertical/used-car-dealer): Used Car Dealer vertical — listings, FRSC verification, AI valuation (M12)"`. PR checklist: P13 VIN exclusion explicitly tested, Tier 3 KYC, `primary_pillars: ["ops","branding","marketplace"]`.

## TASK V-COMM-EXT-C15: Water Tanker / Sachet Water Producer Vertical

- **Module / vertical:** `packages/verticals` + vertical slug `water-vendor`
- **Priority:** P3-Specialist — Nigeria's water supply gap creates huge markets for tanker water delivery and sachet water (pure water) production; NAFDAC regulation is critical
- **Milestone target:** M11
- **Primary pillars:** Pillar 1 (Ops) + Pillar 3 (Marketplace)
- **`primary_pillars` value:** `["ops","marketplace"]`
- **GitHub context:** (standard links)

---

You are an expert **Senior Full-Stack Engineer** specialising in water supply and food production management, working on WebWaka OS.

**Skills required:** Nigerian water vendor market — water tanker delivery (Abuja, Lagos, Ibadan), sachet/bottled water production (NAFDAC permit mandatory), state water board awareness; volume in integer litres; price in kobo per litre or per bag.

---

**1. Mandatory context reading:** All standard documents.

---

**2. Online research and execution plan:**

Research tasks: Nigerian water market — tanker water vs sachet water demand, NAFDAC sachet water permit process, average price per 50cl sachet vs 1,000-litre tanker delivery; key features: tanker delivery log (client, volume_litres INTEGER, price_kobo, delivery_date, status), sachet water production batch log (batch_date, bags_produced INTEGER, production_cost_kobo, selling_price_per_bag_kobo), NAFDAC permit tracking, state water board registration.

Execution plan:
- **Schema:** `water_vendor_profiles` (business_name, type (tanker/sachet/both), NAFDAC_permit, state_water_board_reg, workspace_id, tenant_id, status); `water_deliveries` (client_phone, address, volume_litres INTEGER, price_kobo, delivery_date, status (scheduled/delivered/paid), tenant_id); `sachet_water_batches` (batch_date, bags_produced INTEGER, production_cost_kobo, price_per_bag_kobo, bags_sold INTEGER, tenant_id)
- **FSM:** `seeded` → `claimed` → `nafdac_verified` → `active` → `suspended`
- **SuperAgent:** `DEMAND_PLANNING` (seasonal water demand) — L1; Growth plan
- **KYC:** Tier 1 for tanker delivery; Tier 2 for sachet water production operations (NAFDAC-regulated food production)

---

**3. Implementation:** Branch `feat/vertical-water-vendor`. `registerWaterVendorVertical()` with `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`. Volume as integer litres. All monetary in kobo (P9). Tenant scoped (T3). L1 AI. USSD excluded (P12).

---

**4. QA:** Register; FSM to `nafdac_verified`; delivery with integer volume_litres, price_kobo; sachet batch with integer bags_produced, production_cost_kobo, price_per_bag_kobo; AI demand with aiRights + NDPR; float volume rejected; fractional kobo rejected; cross-tenant 403; AI triply blocked; invalid FSM typed error. Unauth 401; KYC 403. **Minimum: 15 tests.**

---

**5. Finalize and push:**

```bash
git commit -m "feat(vertical/water-vendor): Water Vendor vertical — tanker delivery, sachet batches, NAFDAC compliance (M11)"
git push origin feat/vertical-water-vendor
```
PR checklist:
- [ ] Vertical registered in FSM (with `nafdac_verified` state)
- [ ] Migration created correctly — all volume fields as integer litres (no floats)
- [ ] `primary_pillars: ["ops","marketplace"]`
- [ ] PR labeled: `3in1:pillar-1`, `3in1:pillar-3`
- [ ] Tier 1/2 KYC gate as appropriate
- [ ] AI routes use SuperAgent burn engine at L1 autonomy
- [ ] USSD exclusion enforced (P12)
- [ ] T3 enforced on all D1 queries
- [ ] Tests ≥ 15, all passing
- [ ] Zero typecheck errors (`pnpm -r typecheck`)
- [ ] Zero lint errors (`pnpm -r lint`)
